methodology: Pricing rebuilt against PWM 2024 reality: Security Officer basic floor S$2,938/mo (with allowances ~S$3,200/mo employer cost) + CPF/SDL/insurance/training overhead ~25% + agency margin 18-25% = customer-billed mid-market PWM-compliant rate S$3,400-3,800/guard/mo. Premium tier1 S$4,000-5,000. Sub-S$3,200 quotes are typically PWM-non-compliant or operating at sub-tender margin (high attrition risk). Sources: PSD/GeBIZ tender awards, PLRD Grade A/B/C licence registry, MOM Progressive Wage Model (PWM 2024-2028 trajectory S$3.5k→S$4.4k basic by 2028), competitor public sites where available. Most rates are 'contact for quote' so estimates flagged hidden_estimated. Triangulated against Elitez Security's deck-stated positioning: PWM-compliant mid-premium with documented manpower-reduction case studies (6 sites, $14.6k/mo aggregate savings) backed by 24/7 command centre + group HR depth (Adept Academy + Elitez parent + Hey Rocket + NTUC USE). Aviation-specialist and robotics categories removed from the comparison set: Elitez Security has no aviation arm and no robotics partnership in the deck; their tech is AI-CCTV, LPR, VMS, virtual clocking, anti-exhaustion MDVR, modular guard houses.

categories.tier1_premium: Sovereign-backed or MNC-scale agencies serving government, airports, malls, MNC HQs. S$4,000-5,000/guard/mo. Bureaucratic, slow to quote SMEs.
categories.mid_market_guard: PLRD Grade A/B agencies with 500-2,500 guards, serving MCSTs, commercial buildings, schools, light industrial. S$3,400-3,800/guard/mo PWM-compliant. Direct Elitez Security competitive set.
categories.event_boutique: Specialist crowd-control, F&B venue, concert, conference security. Per-event pricing (S$28-48/guard/hr). Rarely run long contracts.
categories.tech_ai_robotics: Robotics, video analytics, access control, SOC-as-a-service vendors. Sell hardware + SaaS, partner with guard agencies. Hardware CapEx S$30K-150K per unit; SaaS S$200-800/camera/mo.
categories.facility_mgmt: Integrated FM conglomerates with security bolt-on. Security 15-25% of bundle; rarely best-in-class but bundled procurement wins.
categories.group_hr_synergy: Agencies competing on group-backed recruitment + training depth (rare — most SG agencies are stand-alone manpower body shops).
categories.diy_alt: Gig-style or direct-hire platforms (MySwak, Staffany, direct MOM hire). Avoids agency margin but shifts compliance burden to end customer.
categories.budget_subcontractor: Small Grade B/C agencies, often subcontractors. S$2,800-3,200/guard/mo (often PWM-borderline). High turnover, basic service only.

competitors.name: Certis Group
competitors.target_market: SG government contracts (ICA, SPF auxiliary), Changi Airport, Temasek-linked portfolio, enterprise malls (CapitaLand, MapleTree)
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; GeBIZ awards suggest S$4,200-5,000 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Largest auxiliary police force in SG with Mozart AI orchestration platform, armed capability, integrated cash-in-transit.
strengths: Temasek ownership and government access monopoly
Armed auxiliary police — only 3 agencies authorised in SG
Mozart SOC platform integrates guards + tech + cash
weaknesses: Rigid procurement — no PWM-cost-out consultative selling

specialisation_for_cell.industrial_mnc__single_vendor_admin: Master-vendor for Temasek-linked industrial portfolios; one-contract muscle but premium-priced.
specialisation_for_cell.commercial_building__command_centre_24_7: Mozart command centre at scale — but priced for Grade-A landlords only.

competitors.name: Aetos Holdings
competitors.target_market: SG government events (F1, Airshow, NDP), Surbana Jurong properties, ICA border, major transport nodes
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; tender data S$4,000-4,700 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Event-security specialist with 5,000+ multi-skilled workforce and counter-UAV capability, Surbana Jurong-backed.
strengths: Major event security pedigree (F1, Airshow, NDP)
Surbana Jurong parent = infrastructure project pipeline

specialisation_for_cell.event_adhoc__no_double_shift: F1/NDP event-grade SOPs, but freelance pool for surge — double-shift discipline weaker than a steady mid-market roster.

competitors.name: Prosegur Singapore
competitors.target_market: MNC Singapore HQs, European bank branches, high-value cash clients
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; industry benchmark S$3,900-4,400 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global security MNC with iSOC integrated operations centre and dynamic (patrol-based) guarding model.
strengths: Global threat intelligence backbone (150K staff worldwide)
weaknesses: Limited local brand awareness vs Certis/Aetos
Small SG footprint (~500 staff) limits scale deployments

specialisation_for_cell.industrial_mnc__manpower_reduction_proof: iSOC dynamic-guarding case studies for MNC plants — direct overlap with our manpower-cut narrative.

competitors.name: Securitas Singapore
competitors.target_market: Nordic/European MNCs in SG, pharma/semiconductor plants, data centres
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; benchmark S$4,000-4,500 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Swedish-heritage data-driven security with strong remote-guarding technology and ESG credentials.
strengths: Remote guarding reduces 24/7 cost by ~50% for low-risk sites
weaknesses: Passive local marketing — hard to discover
Thin SG bench for large deployments

specialisation_for_cell.industrial_mnc__ai_cctv_lpr_vms_bundle: Securitas remote-guarding platform with mature analytics — credible AI-CCTV story for MNC pharma/semicon.

competitors.name: G4S Singapore (Allied Universal)

competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; benchmark S$4,000-4,500 per guard/mo

specialisation_for_cell.industrial_mnc__single_vendor_admin: Global master agreements pull MNC HQ work into SG ops — but only when the parent company already buys G4S globally.
